element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Required before any game mode; establishes gun-to-screen accuracy baseline
Foundational targeting; blocks move predictably left-to-right; two shots per block allows learning window

element_introduction_order.element: Falling blocks with color mechanics (Blastris B)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Adds spatial reasoning; shooting changes block color; metallic blocks are immutable
Time-pressure introduced; reaction speed rewards accuracy; red moles increase difficulty mid-game
Requires depth perception; missiles approach at varying ranges; miss limit (5) enforces precision
Final challenge mode; presumably highest difficulty

typical_relief_triggers: Clearing a line in Blastris A (blocks disappear, field resets)
Eliminating all moles in Mole Patrol stage (time pressure ends)
Successfully intercepting missiles in Intercept (threat removed)
Completing a level in 30-level LazerBlazer modes
typical_tension_triggers: Blocks approaching left edge in Blastris A (game-over boundary)
Red moles appearing in Mole Patrol (clock acceleration)
Missiles closing distance in Intercept (depth-judgment window narrowing)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Menu-based game selection allows player-controlled pacing between modes; within modes, tension escalates via time limits (Mole Patrol), boundary proximity (Blastris A), and distance judgment difficulty (Intercept)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Calibration and Blastris A introduce static, predictable targets with generous shot economy (2 shots per block)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Blastris B adds color-matching spatial reasoning; Mole Patrol introduces reaction-time pressure with time limits
difficulty_curve.late_game: LazerBlazer modes escalate through 30-level progressions with depth judgment (Intercept) and enemy patterns (Engage/Confront); strict miss limits (5 allowed in Intercept) enforce precision
notable_spikes: Red moles in Mole Patrol Stage Game (clock acceleration mid-stage)
LazerBlazer Intercept depth-judgment mechanic (requires sustained 3D spatial reasoning)

notable_level_patterns.description: Three puzzle-game variants (Tetris-like, Columns-like, Whac-A-Mole-like) using gun peripheral; emphasize spatial arrangement and reaction timing rather than reflexes
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Blastris A Level 1 (Low difficulty): 7×10 grid, blocks scrolling left-to-right, 2-shot allowance per block, 5-line clear per level

notable_level_patterns.description: Three escalating shooter modes sharing 30-level progression structure; early levels teach mechanic, later levels compound difficulty via enemy density, patterns, or judge requirements
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Intercept Type A: Incoming missiles at varying depths; player judges distance to fire on-time; 5-miss limit

notable_level_patterns.description: Whac-A-Mole variant with two modes: Stage Game has hard time limits and red moles that accelerate clock; Score Mode is single-level, score-focused
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mole Patrol Stage Game Level 1: All moles must be shot before time expires; red moles speed up clock, increasing pop-up frequency
